The paper's singleton claim (intersection equals a unique point) requires the specific NP/OA instances derived from the geometric lobe-lattice + meta-witness-depth machinery, deferred to future waves; the structural characterisation here is unconditional and provides the scaffold those future waves will populate. Sanity: TrivialDefectSystem 1-element toy instance (defect_level n = Unit) verifies the scaffold compiles end-to-end; trivial_sigma_invariance theorem confirms the σ-invariance lemma applies on the toy.

name: HolEndMorphism (abstract σ-equivariant endomorphism)
summary: Wave 13 abstract HolEnd_τ(ω) morphism type on a DefectInverseSystem. Packages: act : D.Thread → D.Thread (action on threads), preserves_sigma_fixed (paper Prop 5.6 σ-equivariance as a preservation-of-σ-fixedness field, equivalent to σ(f(G)) = f(σ(G)) but cleaner in Lean). Companion HolEndMorphism.actSigmaFixed lifts the action to SigmaFixedThread using the preservation field. HolEndMorphismFull extends with preserves_NP and preserves_OA for the paper's §5.2 / §5.3 halves, enabling preserves_crossingPoint and universal_fixed_theorem chains. Generic over DefectInverseSystem + anchor / mwd parameters; concrete geometric instantiations plug in as data fields.

name: Fixed-Point Inheritance under σ-Equivariance
summary: Wave 13 paper Proposition 5.6 (prop:fixed-inheritance): for every HolEnd_τ morphism f and σ-fixed thread t, f.actSigmaFixed t is σ-fixed. Direct from HolEndMorphism.preserves_sigma_fixed applied to t.sigma_fixed. Structurally: codomain of actSigmaFixed is SigmaFixedThread, so σ-fixedness is built in — the theorem is the extraction of the built-in property. Companion theorem actSigmaFixed_commutes_proj lifts the σ-commutes-with-projection property through the action, realising the 'passage to the inverse limit preserves σ-invariance' content of the paper §4.2 proof at the HolEnd_τ action level. Unconditional.

name: Universal Fixed Theorem (conditional form)
summary: Wave 13 paper Theorem 5.7 (thm:universal-fixed): G_×[ω] is a universal fixed object under HolEnd_τ(ω). Rendered in conditional form: given the singleton uniqueness of NP ∩ OA as a hypothesis (a future wave deliverable from geometric lobe-lattice + meta-witness-depth infrastructure), every HolEndMorphismFull fixes any crossing-point germ. Proof chain: (a) preserves_crossingPoint (f preserves IsCrossingPoint via preserves_NP + preserves_OA); (b) singleton uniqueness (hypothesis) forces f.act g = g. Companion preserves_crossingPoint theorem is unconditional; the conditional-on-singleton form of universal_fixed becomes unconditional once future waves supply concrete anchor/mwd instances with provable singleton.

name: Universal Fixed Scalar (paper Cor 5.8)
summary: Wave 13 paper Corollary 5.8 (cor:iota-universal): the master constant ι_τ := Read_F(G_×[ω]) is fixed by the induced action of every f ∈ HolEnd_τ(ω) on Read_F(H_τ(ω)). Rendered at the abstract level via universal_fixed_scalar (TauReal form) and threadReadout_universal_fixed (pointwise per-depth form). Companion iota_tau_universal_fixed_structural bridges to Wave 7's CrossingPointDefectGerm and Wave 4's operational TauReal.iota_tau, parametrised over an approximation-level identification hypothesis. Together with singleton_uniqueness (future wave) and the intersection theorem, closes the H3 structural arc at the universal-fixed-scalar level. Sanity: TrivialHolEndMorphism on TrivialDefectSystem compiles end-to-end and trivial_fixed_point_inheritance/trivial_actSigmaFixed_identity verify the scaffold.

name: TorusDefect (Concrete Defect Type)
summary: Wave 14 concrete minimal off-diagonal defect carrier: three-constructor inductive type {bSide, cSide, crossing} realising paper §4's finite torus defect structure. bSide/cSide encode the two lobe off-diagonal positions; crossing is the distinguished σ-fixed anchor. Companion TorusDefect.sigmaSwap : TorusDefect → TorusDefect swaps lobe sides and fixes crossing (paper σ_n as B↔C lobe exchange). Properties proved: sigmaSwap_involutive (σ² = id), sigma_fixed_iff_crossing (crossing is the unique σ-fixed defect element, paper's §5.1 unique σ-fixed boundary locus realised minimally). Design lesson: custom inductive (rather than Option (Fin 2)) avoids Fin-destructuring pattern-match pitfalls in subsequent proofs.

name: TorusDefectSystem σ-fixed thread uniqueness
summary: Wave 14 concrete uniqueness theorem: every σ-fixed thread on TorusDefectSystem equals the crossingThread. Proof chain: (a) sigma_fixed_thread_pointwise_crossing — σ-fixedness at each depth forces point n = crossing (from sigma_fixed_iff_crossing applied to each depth); (b) SigmaFixedThread.ext — pointwise-equal σ-fixed threads are equal (structural extensionality via funext on the point field, Prop-irrelevance on compat/sigma_fixed). Establishes the concrete singleton character of σ-fixed threads on this instance, and packages a clean extensionality lemma (SigmaFixedThread.ext) reusable for any DefectInverseSystem instance.

name: Torus Singleton Uniqueness (Wave 13 Hypothesis Discharged)
summary: Wave 14 litmus test: discharges Wave 13's conditional 'singleton uniqueness of NP ∩ OA' hypothesis unconditionally on the concrete TorusDefectSystem instance. Proof: sigma_fixed_thread_is_crossing (I.T75) forces both crossing-point thread candidates to equal crossingThread, hence to each other. Demonstrates that Wave 13's conditional universal_fixed_theorem becomes UNCONDITIONAL once the concrete geometric uniqueness can be proved, establishing the pattern that richer geometric instances (refinement-growing torus, concrete lobe-lattice) will follow. Companion crossingThread_is_crossingPoint proves the crossing thread satisfies IsCrossingPoint for the concrete torusAnchor / torusMwd parameters.

name: Unconditional Universal Fixed on TorusDefectSystem
summary: Wave 14 sharpest litmus test: Wave 13's universal_fixed_theorem applied to the concrete TorusDefectSystem with singleton_uniqueness discharged via I.T76, yielding an UNCONDITIONAL theorem (no structural hypotheses remain). Chain: (a) TorusIdentity HolEnd morphism (identity action, trivially preserves σ-fixedness + NP + OA); (b) TorusIdentityFull packages NP/OA preservation fields; (c) universal_fixed_theorem applied with torusSingletonUniqueness hypothesis discharged. Companion universal_fixed_scalar_unconditional gives the scalar-readout form; fixes_crossing_thread specialises to the concrete crossing thread computationally. Demonstrates the structural framework is end-to-end coherent: Wave 12 abstract scaffold + Wave 13 conditional theorems + Wave 14 concrete instance = unconditional theorems on the instance.

name: Finite-Stage Coupling Approximants
summary: Wave 15 paper §6.1 (def:approximants) finite-stage approximants. Four depth-indexed Nat → TauRat functions realising paper's four canonical invariants at each depth n: twoApproxAt (dyadic Ger2^(n) = 2), piApproxAt (π-series partial sum GerPi^(n) via Wave 3c), eApproxAt (e-series partial sum GerE^(n) via Wave 3b), iotaApproxAt (operational GerIota^(n) via Wave 4's div-based TauReal.iota_tau). Companion finiteStageEpsilon : Nat → TauRat captures paper Lemma 6.2's correction term ε_n = iota^(n) · (pi^(n) + e^(n)) − 2 as an explicit computable TauRat. Numerical #eval smoke tests at depths 5, 10, 20 confirm iotaApproxAt approaches 0.341 and finiteStageEpsilon evaluates to 0 exactly past Wave 4's boundedAwayFromZero modulus.

name: Ger2 Constant Dyadic Clock
summary: Wave 15 paper §6.1 Equation (6.1-1) / eq:two-n: Ger2^(n) = 2 at every depth n. The dyadic refinement quotient |B_{n+1}|/|B_n| is constant 2 by paper's Definition of 2_τ. At the TauReal level this is TauReal.two being the constant Cauchy sequence at 2 (from Wave 4's TauRealIotaTau infrastructure), whose .approx n evaluates to 2 exactly at every index n via TauReal.two_approx_toRat.

name: Finite-Stage Normalisation Identity at toRat
summary: Wave 15 paper Lemma 6.2 (lem:finite-normalisation) Equation (6.2-1) at toRat level: at every depth n, (iota^(n) · (pi^(n) + e^(n))).toRat = 2 + ε_n.toRat. Proved by unfolding finiteStageEpsilon definition + rewriting via toRat_sub + twoApproxAt_toRat_eq_two + Rat ring identity (a = 2 + (a - 2)). The toRat projection captures the paper's identity content at Rat level; full TauRat struct-level equality would require the complete ring-axiom framework.

name: Coupling Identity at the ω-limit
summary: Wave 15 paper §6.3 Theorem 6.3 (thm:coupling-identity): the coupling identity in the ω-limit, iota_τ · (pi_τ + e_τ) ≡ 2 at Cauchy equivalence on TauReal. Paper's proof passes the finite-stage normalisation through the inverse limit; TauLib rendering inherits Wave 4's operational capstone (iota_tau_mul_pi_plus_e_eq_two, which uses the div-based TauReal.iota_tau := 2/(pi+e) + mul-inv-cancel at the pointwise level). Two proof paths to the same conclusion; the Wave 4 operational path is the one actually exported. Closes the H3 §6 structural arc at the Cauchy-equivalence level.

name: Finite-Stage ε_n Convergence
